speaker: Davicho Barona
text: sometimes a third pass, fourth pass even, to try to understand if it matches your direction, better than the first video. speaker: Davicho Barona
text: No. It is… it will… it is board… it is… a single board has the… has the context that is in that board. Once you create a new board, you can't reference a different board inside of that board.

speaker: Martin Taylor

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: things built in to maximize that speed. Like, for example, you can see these images are blurred out, so I don't have to render all the time. Only when I go in do they start rendering. So the boards are pretty fast, regardless of how many assets I'm keeping in here. Yeah.

This is, like, one shot, and then going into the next view here.

text: and I wanted her to, like, you know, maybe step in front of the camera, take down her, her bandana, in this case, and then face the camera. I would literally just be selecting

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: the two keyframes that I want to animate, and then I would just say, you know, create a keyframe

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: video here, and then I may say using, you know, Veo 3.1 if I want to be, like, an 8-second video. Additionally, something else that I didn't talk about is

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: I can… if I… I can right-click, and right-click brings a whole other menu here, which allows me to either make video, make images, modify the image, I can blend these images, I'm not sure if this is available to you guys yet.

text: I can label them, I can… I can do different things here with this right-click video, as well as, when I click on assets, you're gonna actually see this little…

speaker: Davicho Barona

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: control something. However, you can also just select a bunch of different images in order.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: the order of selection really matters. This is image 1, image 2, image 3, image 4, and you can reference them that way, like, you know, create…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: a, A video using all these

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: Keyframes in succession, or something like this?

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: And it will also…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: there's another tool called Remotion that's built in here that will also stitch videos together. So, I'm not just making videos according to the set length.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: that each, each video generator has, right? Like, for example, Vero 3.1, 4 seconds, 6 seconds, or 8 seconds, etc. All the other video, generators have different limits.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: But you can also stitch videos together. So if you tell it, like, if you grab every single image, and you tell it to make a video out of all of it in a sequence, or in a certain order, or just in a certain way, it will also be able to

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: Talk to it just as if you were bringing all this material, literally, from your office to your, you know, editor's office, or whatever, and you're giving them the assets, and then you're telling it, hey, this is what you need to do with all of this.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: The more detail and context you give it, the better detail and context is gonna come back to you. So, your instructions really matter. Like, good in, good out. But then, you can…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: re… you can iterate on that, right? Like, the same way as you would give it to your assistant or your production team, they come back to you with something.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: That you don't like, then maybe you missed a certain bit of direction that you didn't give them, or there's just something you need to give them feedback on for them to fix, etc.

speaker: Davicho Barona

text: Any questions?

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: That you may need

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: answers to before generating. Like, things like this, like, basically check me. You know, like, tell me what it is that you need before you start creating stuff.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: Like, am I giving you enough information? Am I missing something that you need before you start creating? Same, like, again, think of this as, like.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: a group of agents that are literally there as your production artists, or your assistants, and the more you treat them that way, the better they perform, you know? And so, the same way you would be… you would be giving instructions, feedback, direction.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: To your… to your human team. Treat this the same exact way, and you'll have a much better time.
